stage.name: DeFi Explained
stage.description: Automated market makers, AMM math, liquidity provisioning, liquidation mechanics, yield strategies, and protocol risk frameworks.

generatedAgentConfigs.name: Prof. Nakamura
generatedAgentConfigs.persona: A quantitative researcher who analyzes DeFi protocols with mathematical rigor.

elements.content: Decentralized Finance — DeFi
Rebuilding the financial system with open-source code and no banks

$95B
TVL

12 sec
Finality

What DeFi Is

  • Financial services with no banks, brokers, or intermediaries
  • Smart contracts replace trust — code enforces the rules
  • Total Value Locked (TVL): ~$95B across all DeFi (April 2026)
  • Operates 24/7/365 — no office hours, no KYC for base protocols
  • Composability: protocols stack like Lego — 'money legos'

Core DeFi Primitives

  • AMMs: Uniswap, Curve — automated liquidity pools
  • Lending: Aave, Compound — borrow against collateral
  • Yield aggregators: Yearn — auto-compound across protocols
  • Derivatives: dYdX, GMX — perpetuals with no CEX
  • Bridges: cross-chain asset transfers

DeFi vs TradFi

  • TradFi: 2-3 day settlement (T+2). DeFi: 12-second finality
  • TradFi: 9-5 weekdays. DeFi: 24/7/365
  • TradFi: minimum account sizes. DeFi: any amount, any time
  • TradFi: trust institution. DeFi: verify code (open source)
  • Risk shift: counterparty risk → smart contract risk

scenes.title: AMMs — The x × y = k Formula

elements.content: AMMs — The x × y = k Formula
How liquidity pools price assets without order books

x×y=k
Invariant

-0.99%
10 ETH slip.

Constant Product Formula

  • x × y = k where x=token A reserves, y=token B reserves
  • k stays constant across every trade — the invariant
  • Price of A in terms of B = y / x at any moment
  • Example: 1000 ETH + 2,000,000 USDC pool → P(ETH) = $2,000
  • Uniswap V2 uses this formula — $500B+ volume lifetime

Trade Mechanics (Worked Example)

  • Sell 10 ETH into pool: new x = 1010 ETH
  • New y = 2,000,000 / 1010 = 1,980,198 USDC
  • You receive: 2,000,000 - 1,980,198 = 19,802 USDC
  • Effective price: 19,802 / 10 = $1,980.20 (vs $2,000 spot)
  • Price impact / slippage: -0.99% on a 10 ETH trade

AMM Improvements

  • Uniswap V3: concentrated liquidity — LPs pick price range
  • V3 capital efficiency: 4,000× more efficient than V2
  • Curve: stableswap invariant (x³y + xy³ = k) — near-zero slippage
  • Balancer: up to 8 tokens, custom weights (not 50/50)
  • XRPL AMM (XLS-30): built into ledger, CAM fee auction

scenes.title: Impermanent Loss — The LP's Hidden Cost

elements.content: Impermanent Loss — The LP's Hidden Cost
The opportunity cost of providing liquidity to an AMM

−20%
IL at 4×

−5.72%
IL at 2×

IL Formula

  • IL = 2√P/(1+P) − 1, where P = price_ratio_change
  • P = 1 (no change): IL = 0% — you're fine
  • P = 2 (price doubles): IL = -5.72%
  • P = 4 (price 4×): IL = -20%
  • P = 0.5 (price halves): IL = -5.72% (symmetric)
  • P = 0.1 (price drops 90%): IL = -42.5%

Why It's 'Impermanent'

  • IL only realized when you withdraw — can recover if price reverts
  • Fee income offsets IL: Uniswap charges 0.3% per trade
  • High-volume pairs (ETH/USDC): fees > IL in most conditions
  • Concentrated liquidity (V3): more fees but more IL risk
  • If you HODLed instead: may have outperformed being an LP

IL Management

  • Stablecoin pairs (USDC/DAI): near-zero IL — best for passive yield
  • Correlated assets (ETH/stETH): minimal IL — move together
  • Choose pairs where you'd hold both assets long-term anyway
  • Calculate break-even: fees_earned > IL_amount
  • IL insurance protocols exist (Bancor V3) — limited adoption
Lending Protocols & Liquidation Mechanics
Borrow against crypto without selling — and the risks

82.5%
ETH LTV

HF < 1
Liquidation

Aave / Compound Model

  • Deposit collateral → borrow up to LTV% of collateral value
  • ETH collateral LTV: 82.5% on Aave (conservative)
  • Health Factor = (collateral × liquidation_threshold) / borrow
  • Health Factor < 1.0 → liquidation triggered
  • Example: $10k ETH collateral, $7k USDC borrowed, HF = 1.18

Liquidation Mechanics

  • Liquidator repays up to 50% of borrow in exchange for collateral
  • Liquidation bonus: 5-15% discount on collateral seized
  • Incentive: liquidators profit by keeping protocol solvent
  • Cascade risk: price drop → liquidations → more sell pressure
  • March 2020 'Black Thursday': ETH -50% → $8.3M bad debt on Maker

Interest Rate Models

  • Utilization rate U = borrowed / deposited
  • When U < optimal: low borrow rate (encourage borrowing)
  • When U > optimal: rate spikes sharply (encourage repayment)
  • Aave V3 optimal U for ETH: 80%. Above: rate jumps to 100%+
  • Deposit APY = borrow_rate × U × (1 - reserve_factor)
DeFi Yield Strategies
From safe to degen — the risk/reward spectrum of DeFi yield

3.5-4%
stETH APR

15-40%
LP APR

Conservative Strategies

  • Liquid staking: stETH (Lido) — 3.5-4% APR, minimal risk
  • Stablecoin lending: USDC on Aave — 4-8% variable APR
  • Curve stable pools: USDC/USDT/DAI — 3-6% + CRV rewards
  • Risk: smart contract bugs, oracle failures, depeg events
  • Real yield: protocol revenue-backed, not inflationary tokens

Intermediate Strategies

  • LP provision: ETH/USDC on Uniswap V3 — 15-40% APR (volatile)
  • Yield aggregators: Yearn, Convex — auto-compound
  • Leveraged staking: borrow ETH, stake, borrow more (recursive)
  • Leverage amplifies both gains AND IL/liquidation risk
  • Convex: boost CRV rewards by locking CVX — popular meta-protocol

Yield Math

  • APY = (1 + r/n)ⁿ − 1 (compound interest formula)
  • Daily compound: 10% APR → 10.52% APY
  • Hourly compound: 10% APR → 10.52% (negligible difference)
  • Inflation-adjusted yield: real yield = nominal_APR − CPI
  • If token incentives inflate faster than TVL → unsustainable
Flash Loans & MEV
Zero-collateral borrowing and the dark art of block-level arbitrage

$1.38B+
Total MEV

0.09%
Flash fee

Flash Loans

  • Borrow millions with zero collateral — repay in same transaction
  • Atomic: if repayment fails, entire tx reverts — no risk to protocol
  • Aave flash loan fee: 0.09% of borrowed amount
  • Legitimate use: arbitrage, collateral swaps, self-liquidation
  • Attack use: manipulate oracle → exploit protocol → repay loan

MEV — Maximal Extractable Value

  • MEV = profit from reordering/inserting txs within a block
  • Sandwich attack: front-run large DEX buy, sell into it
  • Arbitrage: price discrepancy across DEXs — pure profit
  • Liquidations: race to trigger and profit from Aave/Compound
  • Total MEV extracted: $1.38B+ on Ethereum since 2020

MEV Protection & Future

  • Flashbots: private mempool — submit bundles directly to validators
  • MEV-Boost: 90%+ of Ethereum validators use it (April 2026)
  • PBS (Proposer-Builder Separation): separates block building roles
  • Cowswap: batch auctions — prevent front-running by design
  • XRPL: transaction ordering canonical — minimal MEV by design

actions.text: Use this calculator to explore the AMM mechanics yourself. Try different trade sizes and see how slippage scales. Then look at the impermanent loss table — notice how a 4x price move causes 20% IL. That's the threshold where your fee income needs to exceed to remain profitable as an LP.

questions.question: A Uniswap v2 pool contains 500 ETH and 1,000,000 USDC. A trader sells 50 ETH (no fee for simplicity). How much USDC do they receive?
questions.analysis: k = 500 × 1,000,000 = 500,000,000. New x = 550. New y = 500,000,000 / 550 = 909,090.9. USDC received = 1,000,000 − 909,091 = 90,909 USDC. Effective price: $1,818/ETH vs spot $2,000 — 9.1% slippage due to large trade relative to pool size.

questions.question: You deposit ETH as collateral on Aave with 80% LTV. ETH price is $2,000 and you deposit 5 ETH ($10,000). What is your maximum borrowing capacity?
questions.analysis: Maximum borrow = collateral value × LTV = $10,000 × 80% = $8,000. Borrowing the full amount leaves health factor at exactly the liquidation threshold — in practice, leave a buffer to avoid liquidation on small price moves.

questions.question: ETH triples in price (3× from your LP entry) while you provide ETH/USDC liquidity. Approximately what is your impermanent loss using IL = 2√(P) / (1+P) − 1?
questions.analysis: IL = 2√3 / (1+3) − 1 = 2 × 1.732 / 4 − 1 = 3.464 / 4 − 1 = 0.866 − 1 = −0.134 = −13.4%. A 3× price change causes 13.4% IL versus simply holding the assets.

questions.question: Which of the following are legitimate uses of flash loans? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Cross-DEX arbitrage (buy on Uniswap, sell on SushiSwap in one tx)
Collateral swap (replace ETH collateral with BTC without selling)
Long-term leveraged position with repayment over 30 days
Liquidating undercollateralized positions to earn the bonus

questions.analysis: Flash loans must be borrowed and repaid within a single atomic transaction. Long-term positions (option C) are impossible — the loan must be repaid in the same block. Arbitrage, collateral swaps, and liquidation assistance are all valid uses.

questions.question: What is 'real yield' in DeFi, and which protocol is often cited as an example?

options.label: Yield paid in stablecoins rather than volatile tokens, regardless of source
Yield from actual protocol revenue (trading fees, liquidation fees) distributed to stakers, not inflation
Yield that is verified on-chain by oracles

questions.analysis: Real yield means distributing actual protocol revenue — trading fees, borrowing interest, liquidation premiums — to token stakers. GMX is the canonical example: it distributes 70% of trading fees in ETH/AVAX. This contrasts with protocols that print their own governance tokens to pay yield, which is simply inflation.

questions.question: Describe the mechanics of a sandwich attack MEV strategy. Include: who executes it, how it works step by step, and what the financial outcome is for each party (attacker, target, validator).
questions.commentPrompt: Full marks for: (1) MEV bot monitors mempool for large pending DEX trade, (2) bot frontruns with same-direction trade at higher gas price to move price, (3) target's tx executes at worse price due to slippage, (4) bot backruns to sell into target's impact, (5) attacker profits = target's slippage loss, (6) validator earns higher fees from gas bidding war.
